page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 124 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled as 539 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity across two drives, while no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark data reveals exceptional single-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in single-threaded tasks, notably in gzip compression at 36.02 GB/sec, bzip3 decompression at 15.11 GB/sec, and Geekbench text processing. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25%. Multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. LLM inference for text generation on small models is highly efficient at 366.16 tokens/sec, though larger models scale down to average tier speeds.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-16-lssd offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring high memory density and fast local storage without the overhead of dedicated GPUs. The inclusion of 539 GB of local NVMe SSD storage reduces the need for external block storage for temporary files or caching. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, text processing pipelines, single-threaded data compression tasks, and lightweight language model inference.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16-lssd is an x86_64 server instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 8 physical cores. It is equipped with 124 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB per core, and includes 539 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While it lacks GPU acceleration, the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and text processing benchmarks. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain in the average tier. Qualitatively, this server provides a cost-effective balance of high memory density and fast local storage for workloads that do not require GPU resources. It is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing pipelines, and single-threaded data manipulation tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16-lssd is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 124 GB RAM, and 539 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.

tagline: A memory-dense x86_64 instance with dedicated Intel Xeon cores and fast local NVMe SSD storage for single-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 124 GB RAM
539 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression speeds
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and data pipelines
